description_long: This is a rectangular wall constructed from dark red and brown bricks arranged in a consistent horizontal pattern. The surface displays the characteristic texture and color variation of fired clay bricks, with visible mortar lines separating each row. The wall is flat and thin, with no visible openings, protrusions, or decorative elements. The brickwork suggests durability and is typical of urban environments such as alleyways, building exteriors, or boundary barriers. The surface appears hard and slightly rough, with a matte finish that absorbs rather than reflects light. The overall condition of the wall is well-maintained, with no visible cracks or damage.
description: A flat, rectangular wall made of dark red and brown bricks in a horizontal pattern, with a hard, matte surface and no visible openings or decorations.
description_view_0: The wall is seen edge-on, appearing as a thin, dark vertical line with no visible brick pattern.
description_view_1: The full brick face is visible, showing the horizontal brickwork and color variation across the surface.
description_view_2: Similar to view 0, the wall is seen edge-on as a thin, dark vertical line.
description_view_3: The brick face is visible again, with the same horizontal pattern and coloration as in view 1.
